name: DNC Rapid Response
subject: A short-sighted, dangerous giveaway to the fossil fuel industry
body:
Take action to help protect our planet.
---
**The Trump administration is going to eliminate the Clean Power Plan -- pitch in $3 now to help elect Democrats who will act on climate change to protect our planet for future generations.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523b35/4067b0a/3b3583777/7e655fc0/1929170840/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20171010_dnc_nd&source=em_20171010_dnc_nd&refcode=em_20171010_dnc_nd")**

The mission of the Environmental Protection Agency is a simple one, Derek: "to protect human health and the environment."

Apparently, no one has shared this information with EPA chief Scott Pruitt.

Pruitt and the Trump administration announced plans to eliminate the Clean Power Plan -- protections that President Obama put in place to limit greenhouse gas emissions and combat climate change.

This is a short-sighted, dangerous giveaway to the fossil fuel industry. If you're sick and tired of this behavior from this administration, this is the time to step up.

**[Pitch in $3 or whatever you can right now to help elect Democrats who will fight back.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523b35/4067b0a/3b3583777/7e655fc1/1929170840/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Pitch+in+3+or+whatever+you+can+right+now&utm_campaign=em_20171010_dnc_nd&source=em_20171010_dnc_nd&refcode=em_20171010_dnc_nd")**

The head of the EPA is tasked with keeping our public lands, water, and air clean and safe. But Pruitt has little interest in doing that job:

* Pruitt was arguably the loudest advocate for leaving the Paris Climate Accord.
* He willfully ignored his agency's own experts and the American Academy of Pediatrics to approve the usage of a pesticide that can cause neurological damage in children.
* In June, Pruitt announced plans to rescind the Clean Water Rule, which keeps streams and rivers that provide drinking water for one-third of Americans safe from dangerous pollutants.
* When he was Oklahoma's attorney general, he made his name suing the EPA to fight regulations on polluters. And he's spent his tenure at the agency holding secret meetings with fossil fuel executives and lobbyists.

This week's news is the latest example of Pruitt doing what's best for the fossil fuel industry at the expense of public health and safety -- the exact opposite of his agency's mission.

Chip in whatever you can right now to help elect Democrats who will keep our air and water safe and protect our planet for future generations.

name: Debbie
subject: enough is enough
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| Friends, In the past 1,740 days, our country has seen more than 1,500 mass shootings. **That breaks down to one mass shooting every nine out of ten days.** _Enough is enough._ We need to do far more than offer thoughts and prayers -- we need our words to be met with action. Earlier this year, we were offering thoughts and prayers for Ft. Lauderdale. A few months before that it was thoughts and prayers for Orlando. These tragedies are heartbreaking and our communities bear the scars from the senseless loss of life that occurred there. We cannot stop the conversation with thoughts and prayers and condolences. It is an abdication of responsibility for members of Congress to mourn and then refuse to take action. **We have a duty to enact common-sense gun safety measures.** We have a duty to ensure that we have done everything in our power to make our communities more safe and secure. These tragedies will continue to happen with a disturbing and devastating frequency until we take action to prevent them from happening. **Inaction cannot be the status quo.** The majority of Americans support common-sense gun safety measures that would save lives. [_**Gun violence is an epidemic in this country and we must take action. Join me and call on Congress to adopt sensible gun safety measures immediately.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e0d/622b7b5b/69b5b16/307bd370/1338684216/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e171009") Thank you, Debbie |

name: The White House
subject: Celebrating Hispanic Heritage Month
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=82f2f4d76d2d37bff54a22660140e528e3ca3be6c069c188c4f03deb5c5130af8cb1535bda24b8044c78ea5aa070ce38898a10064898fb6c

White House Celebrates Hispanic Heritage Month
On Friday, President Donald J. Trump hosted an event to commemorate Hispanic Heritage Month and Hispanic American leaders at the White House. More than 200 Hispanic business, community, and faith leaders from across the country gathered at the White House to hear remarks from President Trump, Secretary Alex Acosta of the Department of Labor, and Treasurer Jovita Carranza from the Department of Treasury.

President Trump discussed the histories, cultures, and many contributions of Hispanic-Americans to our Nation. “Our amazing Hispanic American communities embody our great American values of faith, and family, and security, and hard work, and freedom,” the President said. He also highlighted the contributions of more than a quarter of a million Hispanic Americans serving in the military, “We are all Americans and bound together by this country and this flag,” said the President. “Each of you represents a vital part of the fabric of this Nation.”

Read more about the Hispanic Heritage celebration
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=82f2f4d76d2d37bf7a155a5b9b0476a74f17b51087e714415b19fb652a5e68b8d1f6f1302692b70c5944b0d7fcf61a4ae872399cf6aea698
here.

We Will Get Through This Together
Vice President Mike Pence traveled to Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands on Friday to survey storm damage and meet with members of the community affected by Hurricane Maria. The Vice President visited a local church-turned-relief center, reminding those gathered that they will have the support of the federal government throughout the recovery process. “We will get through this and we will get through this together for everyone,” he said.

Photo of the Day

Vice President Mike Pence visits victims of Hurricane Maria in Puerto Rico | October 7, 2017 (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen)

POTUS AND VP TODAY

This morning, the President received his daily intelligence briefing, followed by a meeting with Dr. Henry Kissinger. In the afternoon, the President had lunch with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson and Secretary of Defense James Mattis. Later, the President welcomed the Stanley Cup Champions: The Pittsburgh Penguins to the White House.

Vice President Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence are in California where they will participate in a briefing on Mojave Air and Space Port and then tour the Virgin Orbit/Virgin Galactic/The Spaceship Company hangar. After that, they will tour the Stratolaunch Systems carrier plane, and then depart for Washington, D.C.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, the President and First Lady will welcome the Prime Minister of Canada Justin Trudeau and Mrs. Gregoire Trudeau to the White House.

Myles Cullen) POTUS AND VP TODAY This morning, the President received his daily intelligence briefing, followed by a meeting with Dr. Henry Kissinger. In the afternoon, the President had lunch with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson and Secretary of Defense James Mattis. Later, the President welcomed the Stanley Cup Champions: The Pittsburgh Penguins to the White House. Vice President Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence are in California where they will participate in a briefing on Mojave Air and Space Port and then tour the Virgin Orbit/Virgin Galactic/The Spaceship Company hangar. After that, they will tour the Stratolaunch Systems carrier plane, and then depart for Washington, D.C. COMING UP Tomorrow, the President and First Lady will welcome the Prime Minister of Canada Justin Trudeau and Mrs. Gregoire Trudeau to the White House.

name: Tim Kaine
subject: The race in Alabama
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

I'm writing to you today about a friend of mine, Doug Jones.

Doug is running to represent the people of Alabama in the U.S. Senate, and I'd like to share with you a little about him. Like me, Doug grew up in a family that put hard work first. While my Dad ran an ironworking shop, his dad worked for U.S. Steel, and his granddads were steelworkers and coal miners. He learned early on to listen to every voice in the conversation -- especially those that others try to drown out. That's why he's spent his entire career fighting against economic and racial injustices.

As U.S. Attorney, Doug took on a decades-old closed case and successfully prosecuted the KKK members responsible for the 16th Street Baptist Church bombing in Birmingham that killed four young African American girls and injured others. And he's bringing that same sense of justice and fairness to the campaign trail every day as a Senate candidate. He's committed to listening to those too often ignored and doing what he can to bring them hope. Honestly, that's what being a senator is all about.

I often feel that listening is a lost art -- not just in politics, but in life. But to be a good leader, you've got to really listen to what folks are saying, no matter which side of the aisle they're on, or whether or not you like what you hear. **I know that Doug is ready to do just that, and that's why I'm supporting him.**

**[I hope you'll join me by chipping in $5 or whatever you can to his campaign.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=45DgJRVejrLaqXnqUt37KWFujag26Vqk)**

This race is winnable. Doug is running against one of the most dangerous Senate candidates I've ever seen -- a candidate who has suggested the horrific tragedy at Sandy Hook was punishment for America's immorality, and that being gay should be illegal. These views are plain scary, and they don't represent the views of the people of Alabama, or America.

It's true that no Democrat has won a major statewide race in Alabama in more than a decade. But when you see an extreme, divisive, downright dangerous candidate like Roy Moore, and when his opponent is a responsible, smart, lifelong Alabamian committed to justice and fairness like Doug... Well, I felt like it was a no-brainer to step up and support Doug. So I hope you'll do the same, because Doug is exactly the kind of Democrat who can win in Alabama.

**[Click here to make an online donation to support Doug Jones in his race for Senate.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=NTAvClbZWurR%2B2s5Mfc1b2Fujag26Vqk)**

Thanks.

Tim Kaine

I'm calling on Congress to act now to avoid jeopardizing the health care of more than 200,000 North Carolina children. Please add your name with me.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12033a6/2fde7c66/4476723b/1569becd/1763204895/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/-add-your-name-to-push-for-health-care-for-north-carolina-children?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC")
---
Friend,

It's easy to get distracted by all the noise coming out of Washington D.C. these days.

I want to make sure you didn't miss this, though.

The Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP) provides North Carolina's working families with affordable, high-quality health insurance coverage for their kids. On September 30, Congress let funding for that program expire.

**[I'm calling on Congress to act now to avoid jeopardizing the health care of more than 200,000 North Carolina children. Please add your name with me.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12033a6/2fde7c66/4476723b/1569becc/1763204895/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/-add-your-name-to-push-for-health-care-for-north-carolina-children?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=2+-+Im+calling+on+Congress+to+act+now+to+avo&utm_campaign=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Has the dysfunction in Washington D.C. really gotten so out of control that we can't get the most basic and necessary programs funded?

The CHIP program provides more than $450 million in health care services to these children each year.

While North Carolina still has funding in the short term, this program is in jeopardy without congressional action.

**[Failing to fund this program is unacceptable. Add your name.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12033a6/2fde7c66/4476723b/1569becf/1763204895/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/-add-your-name-to-push-for-health-care-for-north-carolina-children?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=3+-+Failing+to+fund+this+program+is+unaccept&utm_campaign=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Thanks for all you do,

Roy | | [**Add your name ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12033a6/2fde7c66/4476723b/1569bece/1763204895/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/-add-your-name-to-push-for-health-care-for-north-carolina-children?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=4+-+Add+your+name+rarr&utm_campaign=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_171010_CHIP_adv_gq_ful__RC")

name: Jay Inslee
subject: What Scott Pruitt did is wrong.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Scott Pruitt and Donald Trump just repealed the Clean Power Plan. Here's how we're fighting back.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTIxMTE5NTQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDg1MzIzOQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, I've got a simple question for you: Should it be the job of the EPA Administrator to create federal policy grounded in science, to protect us all from the pollution we know is being pumped into our air from power plants across our nation -- **_OR_** \-- is it his job to spend months meeting with lobbyists for big oil and dirty coal and then repeal common sense policy to protect us from pollution? The answer to that question should be straightforward. But it's not for the Trump administration. Here's what's happening: After years of unmitigated pollution coming from fossil fuel power plants across the country, the Obama administration created the Clean Power Plan to give every state the power to chose their own path forward towards a clean energy future. It was a bold, logical, and effective policy and a critical part of President Obama's strong legacy to help clean up the mess we have made. But after eight months in office meeting almost exclusively with energy special interests, EPA chief Scott Pruitt scrapped Obama's plan today. His decision is shortsighted. It will make our kids sick. Our weather more extreme. It will cost more in the long run to clean up. And it puts each of us in peril. [**That 's why I need your help today -- President Trump and Scott Pruitt need to know we're not going to stand for this. Add your name to my official petition objecting to the EPA's decision to repeal the Clean Power Plan.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIxMTE5NTQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDg1MzIzOQS2&mt=1&rt=0) We should not take this sitting down. While the president and his lackeys might not believe in science, while they might take actions that threaten our health and our air with pollution, I'm working every day to lead us towards a cleaner future. That's why I directed the Washington State Department of Ecology to implement the nation's first Clean Air Rule to limit carbon pollution. That's why Washington is investing in new clean energy technologies to create jobs and protect our water and our forests. And it's why we're partnering with other states and countries to lead on climate action -- even while the Trump administration fails to act. Scott Pruitt and Donald Trump just scored one for big polluters. We know what side they fall on -- so the question is, what are we going to do about it? [**I 'm going to fight for cleaner air and water -- I am going to fight for climate action. If you will, too, start by adding your name to tell Pruitt and Trump you oppose the repeal of the Clean Power Plan in the strongest possible terms.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIxMTE5NTQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDg1MzIzOQS2&mt=1&rt=0) I'm fighting back with actions. I'm fighting back by making Washington a national and global example for how to combat climate change while building the country's top economy. I'm glad to have you on my team for this work. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTIxMTE5NTQS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDg1MzIzOQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

But after eight months in office meeting almost exclusively with energy special interests, EPA chief Scott Pruitt scrapped Obama's plan today. His decision is shortsighted. It will make our kids sick. Our weather more extreme. It will cost more in the long run to clean up. And it puts each of us in peril. We should not take this sitting down. While the president and his lackeys might not believe in science, while they might take actions that threaten our health and our air with pollution, I'm working every day to lead us towards a cleaner future. That's why I directed the Washington State Department of Ecology to implement the nation's first Clean Air Rule to limit carbon pollution. That's why Washington is investing in new clean energy technologies to create jobs and protect our water and our forests. And it's why we're partnering with other states and countries to lead on climate action -- even while the Trump administration fails to act. Scott Pruitt and Donald Trump just scored one for big polluters. We know what side they fall on -- so the question is, what are we going to do about it? I'm fighting back with actions. I'm fighting back by making Washington a national and global example for how to combat climate change while building the country's top economy. I'm glad to have you on my team for this work. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee |

Hello,
Last week, in a cruel step backward for women, House Republicans passed a ban on abortion after 20 weeks.
This bill is now headed for the Senate, and Trump's White House said that it "strongly supports" it. **It puts women 's lives and safety on the line, and we have to do everything in our power to stop it from becoming law.**
[**Will you sign our petition to urge Senate Republicans to protect a woman 's right to make her own health care decisions?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde7a/2a380d31/1d065cc3/fe014b/3147566330/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/2017_stop-20-week-ban?source=e20171010_20weekban_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Just 1% of abortions take place after 20 weeks, and the reasons women have abortions at this stage can be especially complex and heart wrenching:
* Some women learn that their fetus will have underdeveloped organs and won't make it past delivery.
* Some women learn that they themselves are sick and won't be able to get the chemotherapy they need to save their own lives.
* Some women are dealing with geographical hurdles that require them to travel hundreds of miles to a doctor who will perform one.
* Some women come from low-income homes and need time to save up the money needed for the procedure.

No matter the reason, women deserve the chance to make this decision with her doctor, not her government.
[**If you agree, add your name to tell Senate Republicans to stop attacking reproductive health care.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde7a/2a380d31/1d065cc3/fe014b/3147566330/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/2017_stop-20-week-ban?source=e20171010_20weekban_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you for adding your voice,
Team Maggie

Team,

The Trump administration has announced new rules that will allow employers to opt out of providing insurance coverage for birth control.

This announcement means that thousands of women could be forced to pay out of pocket or lose access to necessary health care entirely.

Senate Democrats are already pushing back and they need you to add your voice:

**[Add your name now to oppose the Trump administration's new rules limiting access to birth control.](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954def6/2b07114c/ec86136c/73150329/3969838533/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/protect-birth-control-access?source=PT-20171010-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**

President Obama made huge progress in making birth control more affordable by requiring insurers to pay for all preventative care, including contraception, with no co-pays.

But now, the Trump administration is undermining that progress, allowing employers to opt out of providing coverage.

That's just wrong -- no employer should have the right to interfere with their employees' personal health care choices.

**We can 't allow the Trump administration to roll back the progress we've made. Add your name now to keep birth control affordable for all women.**

Senate Democrats are already pushing back and they need you to add your voice: "=" President Obama made huge progress in making birth control more affordable by requiring insurers to pay for all preventative care, including contraception, with no co-pays. But now, the Trump administration is undermining that progress, allowing employers to opt out of providing coverage. That's just wrong -- no employer should have the right to interfere with their employees' personal health care choices. We can 't allow the Trump administration to roll back the progress we've made. Add your name now to keep birth control affordable for all women. In fact, a full 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We don't have the Koch brothers in our corner -- we have you. subject: Do you live paycheck to paycheck?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
Friend,

Do you live paycheck to paycheck? It's a personal question, but an important one.

If you said "yes," you're not alone. **More than 50% of Americans live paycheck to paycheck.**

This means that most Americans live under constant stress, always wondering if they'll be able to make ends meet.

In a country as great as ours, how did this happen?

**It 's because our current tax system decimates take-home pay. Effectively crippling American workers with unfair and unnecessary taxes.**

As conservatives, we don't believe in high taxes. We believe in individual prosperity -- and that starts with letting you keep more of your paycheck.
**
[If you're ready to lower your taxes and keep more of what you earn, let us know by signing on to support tax reform today >>](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MzA5Njg2MzES1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE0MTMxNzM5MgS2&mt=1&rt=0)**
